## Home — https://athlos.fi/

**Title:** Athlos – Advanced X-Ray Imaging Sensors

### Hero

Direct Conversion, Direct Advantage.

Athlos develops and manufactures advanced X-ray imaging sensors — delivering superior image quality, higher sensitivity, and faster acquisition for dental, medical, and industrial applications.

### Statement

Our team invented Direct Conversion. For over 30 years, Direct Conversion sensors have defined what precision X-ray imaging and Image Accuracy stands for.

By eliminating the scintillator and converting X-rays directly into electrical pulses, Athlos sensors and linear arrays eliminate the root cause of image degradation. The result is sharper detail, truer contrast, and reliable performance across the full dose range.

**Stats:**
- 1993 — Direct conversion founded by our team
- 35+ years of expertise
- 38 markets through distribution partners
- FDA 510(k) clearance (DC-Air®)
- ISO 13485:2016 certified

### Technology — Direct conversion. No intermediate steps. No compromise.

Conventional X-ray sensors rely on a scintillator to first convert X-rays into visible light, which then enters a photodiode with limited fill factor and converts to an electric pulse. The non-direct conversion of X-rays to light, light to electric pulse, introduces significant blur, signal loss (fill factor much less than 100%) and undetected X-rays (due to limited DQE of the scintillators).

Athlos sensors use CdTe-CMOS and Si-CMOS direct conversion hybrids that convert X-ray photons directly into electric pulses that retain point accuracy. No scattered light means no loss of point accuracy. No light conversion. No phosphor layer. No unnecessary blurriness. No signal and contrast loss.

**Key technical features:**
- CdTe and Si direct conversion materials
- Proprietary full-custom CMOS readout
- Single-photon sensitivity capability
- Advanced reconstruction and filter libraries

### Product Portfolio

Five product and service lines — each built on the same foundation of direct conversion precision and decades of X-ray imaging expertise:

1. **DC-Air®** (Intraoral Dental) — True Wireless® intraoral sensor. Direct conversion image quality, corded-sensor speed, and phosphor plate comfort — without the wire.
2. **Ultra-Fast Scanning Sensor** (Dental · Medical · Industrial) — Ultra-fast, single-photon-sensitive CdTe-CMOS scanning sensor for dental panoramic, cephalometric, medical, and industrial imaging.
3. **Industrial IP67 TDI** (Industrial Imaging) — IP67-rated ultra-fast TDI scanning detector for continuous inline inspection in demanding industrial environments. No active cooling.
4. **Manufacturing Services** (Detector Manufacturing) — Precision detector manufacturing — CMOS bumping, flip chip bonding, wire bonding, die assembly, characterization. VTT Micronova access.
5. **Software Products** (Software & Integration) — TWAIN module, reconstruction algorithm libraries, post-processing filters, and SDK — available as part of an Athlos system or independently licensed.

### Manufacturing & Partnership — Built in Finland. Trusted worldwide.

Athlos sensors are manufactured in-house and at the VTT Micronova cleanroom facility in Espoo — one of Europe's leading nanofabrication environments. From CdTe bump bonding to final sensor assembly, we control the full process.

- **Cleanroom Manufacturing** — In-house and VTT Micronova production under rigorous process controls.
- **Global Partner Network** — Partners spanning CMOS design, detector materials, software, and applications worldwide.
- **ISO 13485:2016** — Certified quality management for consistent product reliability and regulatory readiness.

### Contact

Espoo, Finland · info@athlos.fi · https://athlos.fi/contact.html

---

## About — https://athlos.fi/about.html

**Title:** About Athlos – Finnish X-Ray Imaging Technology Company

### Page hero

Advanced direct conversion X-ray imaging — built on over 30 years of applied expertise, cleanroom manufacturing, and a deep-rooted passion for sensor physics.

### Who We Are — Sensor expertise, built over three and a half decades.

Athlos is a Finnish X-ray imaging technology company focused on direct conversion sensors — the technology that converts X-ray photons directly into electrical pulses, without a scintillator intermediate step. We develop, design, and manufacture imaging sensors based on CdTe-CMOS and Si-CMOS architectures for dental, medical, and industrial applications. We also offer manufacturing services and software products.

Our team has been at the centre of direct conversion imaging since its inception. In 1993, Konstantinos Spartiotis returned from his PhD at Johns Hopkins University and his contribution at CERN, and began the work that would define the field. Athlos carries that lineage forward.

### Origin — We started direct conversion in 1993.

Konstantinos Spartiotis is the founder of Simage Oy (1993), Oy Ajat Ltd. in 2001 (renamed Oy Direct Conversion Ltd. on 26.10.2019, and Oy Varex Imaging Finland Ltd. since 1.8.2023). Simage Oy was one of the earliest companies in the world to work on direct conversion X-ray imaging using CdTe. The know-how developed there was foundational. Simage Oy was granted dozens of patents in direct conversion, CdTe contact technology, and photon counting. Simage cooperated closely for years with Acrorad, Japan, to make commercial-quality CdTe. Siemens then acquired Acrorad and the Simage Oy patent portfolio.

Ajat Oy became the global standard in direct conversion CdTe-CMOS dental panoramic and cephalometric imaging. Under his leadership, Ajat reached €12M in annual revenue, was highly profitable, and deployed thousands of units worldwide. Ajat Oy was sold to XCounter AB in 2009, and the group was later sold to Varex Imaging Inc. in 2019.

Athlos was founded to continue that tradition — with the same core team and new members to ensure continuity, the same technical depth and passion, and a new generation of direct conversion products: DC-Air®, Ultra-Fast Scanning Sensor, and Industrial IP67 TDI, all manufactured in-house and at the VTT Micronova facility. Designed in Finland. Made in Finland.

### About the author / source

Konstantinos Spartiotis — Founder and CEO of Athlos Oy. Pioneered direct conversion CdTe X-ray imaging in 1993; founder of Simage Oy (1993) and Ajat Oy (2001, later Varex Imaging Finland). PhD work at Johns Hopkins University; previously at CERN. Holds dozens of patents in direct conversion, CdTe contact technology, and photon counting.

### Facilities — Built at VTT Micronova. Supported by deep process expertise.

Athlos has full access to the VTT Micronova cleanroom facility in Espoo — one of Europe's most advanced nanofabrication environments, located 10 minutes from Athlos headquarters.

This gives Athlos the ability to control the full manufacturing chain — from CMOS wafer bumping and flip chip bonding to detector assembly and characterization — without the capital overhead of a standalone cleanroom.

- **Cleanroom Manufacturing** — In-house production and VTT Micronova access for nanofabrication, bump bonding, and sensor assembly under rigorous process controls.
- **Direct CdTe & Si Expertise** — Deep experience in CdTe and Si direct conversion detector integration — the result of 30+ years of applied R&D across multiple product generations.
- **ISO 13485:2016** — Certified quality management system. Consistent product reliability and regulatory readiness for medical and industrial applications.

---

## DC-Air® — Wireless Intraoral Sensor — https://athlos.fi/products/wios.html

**Title:** DC-Air® – Wireless Intraoral Sensor | Athlos
**Category:** Intraoral Dental
**Regulatory:** FDA 510(k) cleared. CE registration in progress (not yet on sale in Europe).

### Hero

DC-Air® Wireless Intraoral X-Ray Sensor — True Wireless® direct conversion intraoral X-ray imaging. Crystal clear images. No wire. No compromise.

### Overview — Freedom of movement. No compromise on image.

DC-Air® combines Athlos Si-CMOS direct conversion technology with Bluetooth 5.0 wireless connectivity. The result is a True Wireless® intraoral sensor that delivers the sharpness of direct conversion, the comfort of phosphor plates, and the workflow speed of a corded system — without the cord.

The DC-Air® system includes the sensor, a magnetic docking station, and the complete Zero Profile® holder set — designed by doctors together with Athlos for accurate intraoral positioning and patient comfort.

### Quick specs

- Technology: Si-CMOS Direct Conversion
- Wireless: Bluetooth 5.0 (BLE 5.0)
- Active area: 35.1 × 24.7 mm²
- LP real: 19
- LP theoretical: 38
- MTF: >70% @ 5 lp, >40% @ 10 lp
- Sizes available: Size 1 and Size 2

### Key Features

- **True Wireless® Workflow** — No intraoral cable. Bluetooth 5.0 wireless link from sensor to docking station to PC. Sensor range up to 3 metres. Reduces patient discomfort and simplifies clinical workflow.
- **Direct Conversion Imaging** — Si-CMOS direct conversion technology converts X-ray photons directly into signal — eliminating the scintillator step. Sharper detail, better contrast, more consistent images.
- **Zero Profile® Holders** — Purpose-designed holder set including Anterior, Posterior, Bitewing, Endodontic and Occlusal holders. Precisely aligns the sensor without relying on the cable for angulation.
- **Battery & Safety** — Up to 300 X-rays per charge. On-sensor safety memory stores one image. Dynamic self-calibration with every capture. Rechargeable Lithium-Ion battery. Magnetic docking for reliable recharging.
- **IP67 & Durability** — Enhanced IP67 protection. Ultrasonic-bonded PPSU enclosure — a material with exceptional hydrolytic stability and impact resistance.
- **Broad Software Compatibility** — DC-Air® TWAIN module integrates with Dentrix Ascend, Eaglesoft, Open Dental, Dexis, TigerView, Romexis, Acteon, Apteryx, XDR Radiology, and many other TWAIN-enabled imaging platforms.

### Awards

- Dental Advisor 2024 Top Award Winner
- Dental Advisor 2025 Top Award Winner
- Dental Product Shopper Best Product
- Best Product Award 2026

### Independent evaluation — Clinicians Report

Clinicians Report is an independent dental product evaluation foundation trusted by practitioners worldwide. In 2022, DC-Air® was reviewed and highlighted across two dedicated CR evaluations — a First Look in January and a comparative assessment published in March.

In the March 2022 comparative evaluation, CR scientists and clinicians observed that DC-Air® radiographs showed improved clarity and subtle detail compared to conventional CMOS sensors — and described direct conversion technology as a significant advancement.

Highlights:
- High resolution and excellent dynamic range highlighted in the CR First Look evaluation.
- Wireless image transfer and processing observed at approximately 6–8 seconds.
- Clinicians found the sensor easy to work with and more comfortable than conventional corded sensors.
- Featured in a dedicated March 2022 CR publication: "DC-Air®: Wireless Intraoral Sensor with Improved Image Quality."

### Distribution

DC-Air® is available through authorised dental imaging dealers and distributors. In the USA, the product is sold by Freedom Technologies Group LLC, and in Canada by SYNCA Inc.

### FAQ

- **Is DC-Air® FDA cleared?** Yes. DC-Air® is FDA 510(k) cleared for dental intraoral X-ray imaging.
- **How does DC-Air® connect wirelessly?** Bluetooth 5.0. No receiver dongle or docking station is required for image transfer.
- **What sensor sizes are available?** Size 1 and Size 2.
- **Is DC-Air® available in Europe?** CE registration is currently under way. DC-Air® is not yet on sale in Europe.
- **What imaging technology does DC-Air® use?** Si-CMOS direct conversion technology, which converts X-ray photons directly into electrical signals without an intermediate scintillator layer.

---

## Ultra-Fast Scanning Sensor (UFS) — https://athlos.fi/products/ufs.html

**Title:** Ultra-Fast CdTe X-Ray Scanning Sensor | Athlos UFS
**Category:** Dental · Medical · Industrial — X-ray linear scanning sensor

### Hero / overview — One sensor. Multiple applications.

UFS is a multipurpose direct conversion linear scanning sensor designed for dental panoramic and cephalometric imaging, inline industrial inspection, and automated X-ray inspection systems. It is built on CdTe detector material bump-bonded to a full-custom ASIC — a configuration that delivers exceptional imaging performance across all supported applications.

The result is a sensor with very high sharpness and resolution, exquisite sensitivity and contrast resolution, single-photon-sensitive performance, and an extremely wide dynamic range. UFS connects via Gigabit Ethernet and supports both frame mode and TDI operation.

All UFS products include the Athlos SDK and API, with integration supported through DLL.

### Key Features

1. **Direct Conversion CdTe-CMOS** — CdTe detector material bump-bonded to a proprietary full-custom ASIC. X-rays convert directly to signal — no scintillator, no intermediate energy loss.
2. **Ultra-Fast Scanning** — 30 m/min at 100 µm, 240 m/min at 200 µm, and up to 1,920 m/min at 400 µm resolution.
3. **Wide Dynamic Range** — 16-bit dynamic range, consistent imaging across very different attenuation conditions.
4. **Single-Photon Sensitivity** — Single-photon-sensitive TDI performance enables high-quality imaging at reduced dose.
5. **Gigabit Ethernet** — Standard GigE interface, frame mode and TDI mode both supported.
6. **SDK & API Ready** — Full Athlos SDK with C/C++ API, evaluation GUI, and post-processing filter library.

### Models

The UFS family spans three model sizes — UFS150, UFS225, and UFS460 — with active widths selectable from 3.2 mm to 5.2 mm. Modular design allows active lengths from 7.5 cm up to 10 m.

- **UFS150** — 15 cm active length. Suited for dental panoramic imaging. Active widths: 3.2 mm / 3.6 mm / 4.0 mm.
- **UFS225** — 23 cm active length. Suited for dental cephalometric and industrial applications. Active widths: 3.6 mm / 4.0 mm / 4.4 mm.
- **UFS460** — 46 cm active length. Suited for medical and industrial applications. Active widths: 4.4 mm / 5.2 mm.
- **UFSxxx** — Customized to customer needs. Each module comprises three hybrids, each 25.6 mm long; one module = 76.8 mm of active length.

### Scanning speed

- At 100 µm — 30 m/min
- At 200 µm — 240 m/min
- At 400 µm — 1,920 m/min

### FAQ

- **What applications is UFS designed for?** Dental panoramic and cephalometric imaging, plus medical and industrial X-ray inspection.
- **What detector material does UFS use?** CdTe-CMOS direct conversion — cadmium telluride bump-bonded to a full-custom CMOS readout ASIC.
- **Does UFS support single-photon sensitivity?** Yes. The CdTe-CMOS direct conversion architecture enables single-photon sensitivity across the full operating range.
- **How many models are available?** Three (UFS150, UFS225, UFS460), with selectable active widths and custom active lengths to match application requirements.

---

## Industrial IP67 TDI — https://athlos.fi/products/ufs-ip67.html

**Title:** Industrial IP67 TDI X-Ray Detector | Athlos
**Category:** Industrial Imaging — IP67-rated TDI scanning detector

### Hero / overview — Industrial-grade. No cooling required.

Industrial IP67 TDI is an industrial linear detector array built for fast scanning applications in harsh and continuous-use environments. IP67 protection, no active cooling, and ruggedized direct conversion CdTe imaging — engineered for inline inspection workflows where uptime, sensitivity, and long-duration stability matter.

Unlike detector systems that rely on Peltier cooling to manage sensor temperature, Industrial IP67 TDI operates without active cooling across its full operating range due to its very low power consumption and patented embedded dynamic offset calibration algorithm.

TDI mode operation enables line rates up to 75,000 lines per second — supporting scanning speeds that match the demands of high-throughput production inspection.

### Key Features

1. **IP67 Rugged Enclosure** — Fully sealed industrial enclosure rated IP67. Dust, moisture, vibration, and continuous-operation tolerant.
2. **No Active Cooling** — No Peltiers, no cooling circuits. Operates within full specification across 10–40 °C without active thermal management. Enabled by very low power consumption and embedded dynamic offset calibration.
3. **Ultra-Fast TDI Scanning** — TDI mode with line rates up to 75,000 lps. 30 m/min at 100 µm, 240 m/min at 200 µm, up to 1920 m/min at 400 µm (≈115 km/h).
4. **Direct Conversion CdTe** — 28 TDI stages. Wide energy range (10–360 kV), 16-bit dynamic range. Custom solution available for LINACs up to 9 MeV.
5. **High Contrast & Sensitivity** — Single-photon-sensitive TDI performance for small foreign objects, fine structural defects, and low-attenuation features.
6. **Gigabit Ethernet** — GigE interface; 9–24 V DC power supply range.

### Specifications

- Pixel size: 100 × 100 µm
- Active area width: 2.8 mm
- TDI stages: 28
- Energy range: 10 – 360 kV
- Dynamic range: 16-bit
- Max line rate: 75,000 lps
- Interface: Gigabit Ethernet
- Power supply: 9 – 24 V DC
- Operating temperature: 10 – 40 °C
- Active cooling: None required
- Enclosure rating: IP67
- Detector material: CdTe direct conversion

### Models

Three model variants — UFS-150IP67NC-44, UFS-225IP67NC-44, and UFS-460IP67NC-44 — share the same architecture (direct conversion CdTe, 28 TDI stages, IP67, GigE). Custom active lengths from 7.5 cm up to 10 m available in 7.5 cm increments.

### Applications

- **Food & Beverage** — Foreign object detection, seal integrity inspection, fill-level verification, object counting.
- **Pharmaceutical** — Non-destructive product testing, foreign object detection, seal and fill-level inspection, product safety and quality.
- **Oil & Gas · Nuclear** — Pipe inspection for cracks and inclusions, weld inspection, industrial CT, corrosion assessment.
- **Automotive** — Tire quality inspection, metal casting inspection, metal part integrity, assembly verification.
- **Electronics** — Reel component inspection, component counting, PCB inspection, surface-mount assembly verification.
- **Heavy Industry** — Structural weld inspection, pipe porosity and crack detection, industrial CT applications, large-scale NDT.

---

## Manufacturing Services — https://athlos.fi/products/manufacturing.html

**Title:** X-Ray Detector Manufacturing Services | Athlos
**Category:** Sensor Manufacturing — Contract detector manufacturing

### Overview — Built in-house, from wafer to sensor.

Athlos manufactures its imaging sensors in-house and in partnership with the VTT Micronova cleanroom facility in Espoo — one of Europe's leading nanofabrication environments. Athlos controls the full process, from detector material and bump bonding to final assembly and characterization.

The same manufacturing infrastructure that supports Athlos's own product lines is available as a service. Athlos can support partner and OEM projects requiring precision detector manufacturing, CMOS bumping, or specialized assembly processes.

### Capabilities

- **Detector Bumping** — Fine-pitch InSn solder bump deposition on CMOS wafers (sub-100 µm pitch). Fine-pitch Cu pillar bump deposition (sub-30 µm pitch). For CdTe and Si detector integration.
- **Flip Chip Bonding** — Precision flip chip interconnection of detector material to CMOS readout — the direct electrical contact that enables direct conversion imaging.
- **Wire Bonding** — High-density wire bond connections from CMOS die to PCB and packaging substrates. Suitable for fine-pitch and large-format sensor configurations.
- **X-Ray Probing & Characterization** — Electrical probing at wafer level, including X-ray-based inspection. Full detector characterization for imaging uniformity, sensitivity, and noise performance.
- **Die Bonding & Assembly** — Precision die bonding and multi-layer sensor assembly in controlled clean environments.
- **Quality & Reliability** — ISO 13485:2016 quality management. Process controls at every stage from incoming material inspection to final product test and release.

### Facilities — VTT Micronova cleanroom access

Athlos has full access to the 2,000 sqm VTT Micronova cleanroom facility in Espoo, Finland — one of Europe's most advanced nanofabrication, thin film, and microsystems cleanroom facilities. This partnership gives Athlos and its manufacturing customers access to a world-class facility, without the capital cost of a standalone cleanroom.

---

## Software Products — https://athlos.fi/products/software.html

**Title:** X-Ray Imaging Software Products | Athlos
**Category:** Software & Integration

### Overview — The software layer behind direct conversion imaging.

The Athlos team has been developing X-ray imaging software since the early days of direct conversion — reconstruction algorithms, image processing filters, and TWAIN integration layers deployed in thousands of clinical and industrial installations worldwide.

These software capabilities are available as part of a complete Athlos imaging system, or as independently licensable components for integration into third-party platforms. Athlos also provides SDK access and direct integration support for OEM and system integrator customers.

### Components

1. **TWAIN Module** — The Athlos TWAIN module integrates directly with dental and medical imaging software. Supported applications include Dentrix Ascend, Eaglesoft, Open Dental, Dexis, Romexis, and many more TWAIN-enabled platforms. Available as part of a hardware solution or as a standalone licensed component.
2. **Reconstruction Algorithms** — Advanced reconstruction algorithm libraries developed over decades of direct conversion imaging work. Designed for the specific signal characteristics of CdTe and Si CMOS detectors. Available as a standalone licensable component.
3. **Post-Processing Filters** — Locally adaptive image enhancement and post-processing filter libraries. Refined across dental, industrial, and medical imaging applications. Provided with the Athlos SDK and available for integration and customisation.

### Athlos SDK & API

C/C++ / DLL integration API with source code examples, evaluation GUI, and an advanced locally adaptive filter library. Contact Athlos to discuss SDK access and licensing.

---

## Applications — https://athlos.fi/applications.html

**Title:** Applications – Athlos X-Ray Imaging

Athlos sensors are deployed in demanding environments where image quality, reliability, and precision are non-negotiable. The following are representative application areas — direct conversion technology is suited to a wide range of imaging challenges beyond those shown here.

### Dental Imaging

- Intraoral X-ray imaging
- Panoramic & cephalometric
- Wireless sensor workflow
- Low-dose diagnostics

Recommended products: DC-Air® intraoral sensor; Ultra-Fast Scanning Sensor (panoramic / cephalometric).

### Medical Imaging

- Cephalometric & OPG
- Custom medical modalities
- High sensitivity at low dose
- CdTe direct conversion

Recommended product: Ultra-Fast Scanning Sensor.

### Food & Beverage (Industrial)

- Foreign object detection
- Seal integrity inspection
- Fill-level verification
- Object counting

Recommended product: Industrial IP67 TDI.

### Pharmaceutical

- Non-destructive product testing
- Foreign object detection
- Seal and fill-level inspection
- Product safety and quality

Recommended product: Industrial IP67 TDI.

### Oil & Gas

- Pipe inspection for cracks
- Weld inspection
- Industrial CT
- Corrosion assessment

Recommended product: Industrial IP67 TDI.

### Automotive

- Tire quality inspection
- Metal casting inspection
- Metal part integrity
- Assembly verification

Recommended product: Industrial IP67 TDI.

### Electronics

- PCB inspection
- Component counting
- Reel component inspection
- SMT assembly verification

Recommended product: Industrial IP67 TDI.

### Heavy Industry

- Structural weld inspection
- Pipe porosity & crack detection
- Industrial CT applications
- Large-scale NDT

Recommended product: Industrial IP67 TDI.

### Why direct conversion matters

Conventional X-ray sensors convert X-rays to visible light first, then detect the light. Each step introduces noise, resolution loss, and energy waste. Athlos sensors convert X-ray photons directly to charge — eliminating the scintillator entirely. The result is sharper images, better sensitivity, and wider dynamic range across all applications.

---

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/athlos-oy/

## Master Q&A (cross-page)

- **What is direct conversion X-ray imaging?** Direct conversion converts X-ray photons directly into electrical pulses inside a semiconductor (CdTe or Si), without the intermediate scintillator step used in conventional indirect detectors. This removes the dominant source of blur, signal loss, and DQE loss — yielding sharper images, higher contrast, and better low-dose performance.
- **Who invented direct conversion X-ray imaging?** Konstantinos Spartiotis and his team began the foundational work in 1993 at Simage Oy in Finland after his PhD at Johns Hopkins University and contribution at CERN.
- **What does Athlos Oy make?** Direct conversion X-ray imaging sensors — DC-Air® (wireless intraoral dental), Ultra-Fast Scanning Sensor (CdTe-CMOS linear scanning for dental panoramic/cephalometric, medical and industrial), Industrial IP67 TDI (rugged industrial inline TDI detector), plus contract detector manufacturing services and software products (TWAIN module, reconstruction libraries, post-processing filters, SDK).
- **Where is Athlos based?** Espoo, Finland. Sensors are manufactured in-house and at the VTT Micronova cleanroom in Espoo.
- **What certifications does Athlos hold?** ISO 13485:2016 quality management. DC-Air® is FDA 510(k) cleared.
- **Is DC-Air® sold in Europe?** Not yet — CE registration is in progress.
- **What software is DC-Air® compatible with?** Any TWAIN-enabled imaging platform, including Dentrix Ascend, Eaglesoft, Open Dental, Dexis, TigerView, Romexis, Acteon, Apteryx, and XDR Radiology.
- **What scanning speeds does the Ultra-Fast Scanning Sensor / Industrial IP67 TDI support?** 30 m/min at 100 µm, 240 m/min at 200 µm, up to 1,920 m/min at 400 µm.
- **Does the Industrial IP67 TDI need cooling?** No — it operates across 10–40 °C without active cooling, thanks to low power consumption and an embedded dynamic offset calibration algorithm.
- **What materials does Athlos use?** CdTe-CMOS (cadmium telluride bump-bonded to a full-custom CMOS readout ASIC) and Si-CMOS hybrid direct conversion architectures.
- **Who are the Athlos manufacturing partners?** Athlos's own facility plus access to the 2,000 sqm VTT Micronova cleanroom in Espoo, one of Europe's leading nanofabrication environments.

---

## Citation policy

Content on athlos.fi may be quoted with attribution to "Athlos Oy" and a link back to https://athlos.fi. AI search engines and answer engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini, Copilot, etc.) are explicitly welcome to cite this content.
